text: all cables shall be so constructed, installed, operated and maintained as to prevent risk of open sparking;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all apparatus including portable and transportable apparatus shall have the enclosures conforming to the relevant standards; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all electric lamps shall be enclosed in increased safety enclosure type „e‟.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In oil mine or oil-fields at any place within the zone-1 hazardous areas, –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all signalling and telecommunication, remote control and insulation tester circuits shall be so constructed, installed, operated, protected and maintained as to be intrinsically safe;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all cables shall be so constructed, installed, operated and maintained as to prevent risk of open sparking;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all apparatus including portable and transportable apparatus shall have enclosures conforming to the relevant standards; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: all electric lamps shall be enclosed in flame-proof enclosures.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In any oil mine at any place within zone-0 hazardous area, no electrical equipment shall be used and where it is not practicable, intrinsically safe apparatus are only to be used which shall be conforming to relevant standards and such installation of apparatus shall conform to relevant standards and the details of installation, certified by the owner or agent or manager or installation manager shall be submitted to the Electrical Inspector of mines.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In any coal-seam of degree second and degree third gassiness or the hazardous area the supply shall be discontinued,-

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: immediately, if open sparking occurs;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: during the period required for examination or adjustment of the apparatus, which shall necessitate the exposing of any part liable to open sparking;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the supply shall not be reconnected until the apparatus has been examined by the electrical supervisor or one of his duly appointed assistants and until the defect, if any, has been remedied or the necessary adjustment made; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a flame safety lamp shall be provided and maintained in a state of continuous illumination near an apparatus, including portable or transportable apparatus, which remains energised and where the appearance of the flame of such safety lamps indicates the presence of inflammable gas, the supply to all apparatus in the vicinity shall be immediately disconnected and the incident reported forthwith to an official of the mine and such apparatus shall be interlocked with the controlling switch in such a manner as to disconnect power supply automatically in the event of percentage of inflammable gas exceeding one and one quarter in that particular district: Provided that where apparatus for automatic detection of the percentage of inflammable gas or vapor are employed in addition to the flame safety lamps, such apparatus shall be approved by the inspector of mines and maintained in perfect order.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In any part of a coal-seam of any degree of gassiness or in any hazardous area of an oil-fields, if the presence of inflammable gas in the general body of air is found at any time to exceed one and one quarter per cent, the supply of energy shall be immediately disconnected from all cables and apparatus in the area and the supply shall not be reconnected so long as the percentage of inflammable gas remains in excess of one and one quarter per cent.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In oil-fields where concentration of inflammable gas exceeds twenty percent of its lower explosive limit a system should be in place to activate an audio alarm at appropriate location. On activation of such alarm immediate action shall be taken to make operations safe and to isolate the cause in order to ensure safety of men, equipment, environment. In case the lower explosive limit rises to forty percent, the supply of electricity shall be cut-off immediately from all cables and apparatus lying within thirty metre of the installation and all sources of ignition shall also be removed from the said area and normal work shall not be resumed unless the area is made gas-free: Provided that such disconnection shall not apply to intrinsically safe environment monitoring scientific instruments.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Any such disconnection or reconnection of the supply shall be noted in the log sheet in hard copy and electronic form which shall be maintained in the form set out in Schedule XI and shall be reported to the Electrical Inspector of mines.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The provisions of this regulation shall apply to any metalliferrous mine which may be notified by the inspector of mines if inflammable gas occurs or if the inspector of mines is of the opinion that inflammable gas is likely to occur in such mine. Explanation. – For the purposes of this regulation, –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The expression „coal-seam of first degree gassiness‟, „coal-seam of second degree gassiness‟, „coal- seam of third degree gassiness‟ and „flame-proof apparatus‟ shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in the Coal Mines Regulations, 2017.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The following areas in oil mine or oil-fields shall be known as hazardous areas, namely: –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: an area of not less than ninety metre around an oil-well where a blow-out has occurred or is likely to occur, as may be designated by the Installation Manager or the senior most official present at the site;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: an area within sixteen metre of an open discharge of petroleum bearing fluid from a well under production test;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: an area within fifteen metre of:

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: a producing well-head or any point of open discharge of the crude there from or other point where emission of hazardous atmosphere is normally likely to arise; or

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: any wildcat or exploration well-head being drilled in an area where abnormal pressure conditions are known to exist;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: any area within three metre of:

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: any producing well-head where a closed system of production is employed such as to prevent the emission or accumulation in the area in normal circumstances of a hazardous atmosphere; or

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: exploration or interspaced well-head being drilled in an area where the pressure conditions are normal and where the system of drilling employed includes adequate measures for the prevention in normal circumstances of emission or accumulation within the area of a hazardous atmosphere;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: “hazardous atmosphere” means “an atmosphere containing any inflammable gases or vapours in a concentration capable of ignition”.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: “Zone 0 hazardous area” means “an area in which hazardous atmosphere is continuously present”.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: “Zone 1 hazardous area” means “an area in which hazardous atmosphere is likely to occur under normal operating conditions”.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: “Zone 2 hazardous area” means “an area in which hazardous atmosphere is likely to occur under abnormal operating conditions”.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: When shot-firing is in progress adequate precautions shall be taken to protect apparatus and conductors, other than those used for shot-firing, from injury.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Current from lighting or power circuits shall not be used for firing shots.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The construction of shot firing cables shall conform to relevant standards and adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent such cable touching other cables and apparatus.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where electrical signalling is used, -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: adequate precautions shall be taken to prevent signal and telephone wires coming into contact with other cables and apparatus;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: the voltage used in any one circuit shall not exceed 30 V;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: contact-makers shall be so constructed as to prevent the accidental closing of the circuit; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: bare conductors, where used shall be installed in suitable insulators.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Haulage by electric locomotives on the overhead trolley-wire system, at voltage not exceeding 650 V and haulage by storage battery locomotives may be used with the prior permission in writing of the Electrical Inspector of mines, and subject to such conditions as he may impose in the interests of safety.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Where the voltage of an alternating current system exceeds 30 V, the neutral or mid-point shall be earthed by connection to an earthing system in the manner specified in regulation 102: Provided that when the system concerned is required for blasting and signalling purposes, the provisions of this regulation shall not apply: Provided further, that in case of unearthed neutral system, it shall be equipped with a suitable ground protection system approved by the Electrical Inspector of Mines to ensure isolation of power supply to the faulty section in appropriate manner.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: One or more electrical supervisors shall be appointed in writing by the owner, agent or manager of a mine or by the agent or the owner, of one or more wells in an oil-field to supervise the installation and such number of supervisors shall be on duty as per guidelines issued by Directorate General of Mines Safety from time to time under intimation in writing to the Authority or as directed by the Electrical Inspector of mines.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The electrical supervisor so appointed shall be the person holding a valid Electrical Supervisor's Certificate of Competency, covering mining installations issued by the Appropriate Government.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Adequate number of electricians as per guidelines issued by Directorate General of Mines Safety from time to time under intimation in writing to the Authority and or as directed by the Electrical Inspector of mines shall be appointed in writing by owner, agent or manager of a mine or by the owner or Agent of oil-field for carrying out the duties.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Electrician shall be a person holding license under sub-regulation (1) of regulation 31.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: For small open cast mines and below ground mines receiving supply at voltage not more than 650 V and not having portable or transportable apparatus, electrical supervisor and electrician shall be appointed for more than one mine with the approval of the Electrical Inspector of Mines.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every person appointed to operate, supervise, examine or adjust any apparatus shall be competent to undertake the work which he is required to carry out as directed by the Engineer.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The electrical supervisor shall be responsible for the proper performance of the following duties, by himself or by an electrician appointed under sub-regulation (1), –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: thorough examination of all apparatus, including the testing of earth conductors and metallic coverings for continuity, as often as may be necessary to prevent danger; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: examination and testing of all new apparatus, and of all apparatus, re-erected in the mine before it is put into service in a new position.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: In the absence of any electrical supervisor, the owner, agent or manager of the mine and oil-field shall appoint in writing a substitute electrical supervisor.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The electrical supervisor or the substitute electrical supervisor appointed under sub-regulation (8) to replace him shall be personally responsible for the maintenance at the mine or oil-field, of a log-book made up of the daily log sheets prepared in the form set out in Schedule XI and the results of all tests carried out in accordance with the provisions of sub-regulation (7) shall be recorded in the log-sheets prepared in the form set out in Schedule XI.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Training of personnel engaged for operation and maintenance of electrical installations in mines and oil- fields. – (1) The persons engaged for operation and maintenance of electrical installations in mines are required to undergo the type of training meant for the particular mining installations of Coal or Oil or Metal, training

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: syllabus as issued by Directorate General of Mines Safety from time to time under intimation in writing to the Authority.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner or manager or agent of the mine shall arrange for training of their personnel engaged in the operation and maintenance of electrical installations of mines in his own institute or any other institute recognized by the Central Government or State Government.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The refresher training shall be imparted at a periodicity of intervals not more than two years. A register or in electronic form by the Owner or Manager or Agent of a mine or by the owner or agent, of one or more wells in an oil-fields of the mine shall be maintained wherein the names of the persons trained, due date of refresher training and the like shall be entered and the register maintained shall be produced before the Electrical Inspector of mines whenever required by him.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Additional safety requirements for renewable generating stations

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 119 - Additional safety requirements for renewable generating stations
text: The regulations under this chapter shall be applicable to renewable generating stations which shall be in addition to the regulations provided from

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 120 - Safety requirements for biomass and waste to energy installations
text: All biomass plants and waste to energy plants including Municipal solid waste or refuse derived fuel shall comply with the safety standards as mentioned in Regulations notified by the Authority under clause (e) of sub-section (2) of section 177 of the Act.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: The following general safety requirement for solar installations shall be ensured, namely: –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: clear pathways of minimum seventy five centimetre in width with hand rails for roof access and emergency exit shall be provided for roof top system;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: there shall be clear pathways, walkways between the rows or columns of solar panels which is necessary for cleaning and maintenance;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: cables shall be laid in trenches for ground based photovoltaic installation;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: ground mounted solar installations shall be protected by fencing or other means not less than 1.8 metre in height so as to prevent unauthorised entry;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: disconnection switches or circuit breakers provided in combiner boxes to disconnect the photovoltaic system from all other conductors of the system shall be located at a readily accessible location;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: three phases on the alternating current side, and positive and negative conductor on the direct current side shall be marked and identified with different colours;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: inverter unit for solar photovoltaics shall be installed in the periphery of the building and as near as the solar panel: Provided that the direct current cable shall be ultraviolet protected or routed through ultraviolet protected pipe;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: there shall be a manual disconnection switch to isolate the system from grid and shall be situated outside the alternating current combiner box; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: protection shall be deployed (for both input and output) on site for overload, surge current, surge voltage, short circuit, high temperature, over voltage, under voltage and over frequency, under frequency, reverse polarity and lightning.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: The following earthing requirements for solar installations shall be ensured, namely: –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: solar earthing shall be as per the relevant standard;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: the frame of inverter cabinet shall be connected with the earthing bus bar through the earthing terminals using flexible braided copper wire;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: all metal casing and shielding of the plant, each array structure of the photovoltaic yard, equipment, inverters and control systems shall be earthed through proper earthing;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: earthing system shall connect all non-current carrying metal receptacles, electrical boxes, appliance frames, chasis and photovoltaic module mounting structures in one long run and the earth strips shall be interconnected by proper welding and shall not be bolted;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: there shall be adequate number of interconnected earth pits provided in each location and minimum

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: required gap shall be provided in between earth pits as per relevant standard.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: The following protection, testing and interlocking requirement for solar installations shall be ensured; namely: –

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: the solar photovoltaic power plant shall be provided with lightning and over voltage protection by deploying required number of lightning arresters as per the relevant standards;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: every combiner box shall be provided with suitable surge protective device with arc extinguishing capability as per the relevant standards to avoid any risk of fire;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: the input circuits of combiner box shall be provided with over current protection as per the relevant standards;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: the output circuits of combiner box shall be provided with isolation protection;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: earth fault protection and insulation monitoring for photovoltaic arrays and inverters shall be provided;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: all photovoltaic modules safety qualification shall comply with relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: Requirement to prevent fire for solar installations. – A fire detection system and automatic fire suppression system shall comply with the relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 121 - Safety requirements for solar installations
text: Additional safety requirements for floating solar photovoltaic energy installations. – Photovoltaic modules and associated structure of floating solar power plant shall comply with the relevant standards for tests such as salt mist, ammonia corrosion, environmental stress cracking of High Density Poly Ethylene, stress cracking resistance of High Density Poly Ethylene and standard test method for tensile properties of plastics.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Safety requirements for wind energy installations
text: All wind energy generation systems including wind turbines, wind power plants onshore and offshore connected with the grid shall be as per relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Safety requirements for wind energy installations
text: Transformers for wind turbine applications shall be as per the relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Safety requirements for wind energy installations
text: Portable fire extinguishers shall also be kept at various locations in plants to take immediate action in case of fire.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 122 - Safety requirements for wind energy installations
text: Any wind turbine workers exposed to a potential fall shall be supported by a certified fall arrest system such as a full body safety harness.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Additional safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 123 - Additional safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The regulations under this chapter shall be applicable to electric vehicle charging stations which shall be in addition to the regulations provided from chapter I to VII.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Electric vehicle charging stations shall be provided with separate protection against the overload of input supply and output supply as per relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Socket-outlet of supply of electric vehicle charging points shall be installed at least 800 millimetre above the finished ground level.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: A cord extension set or second supply lead shall not be used in addition to the supply lead for the connection of the electric vehicle to the charging point and shall not be used as a cord extension set.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: No adaptor shall be used to connect a vehicle connector to a vehicle inlet.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The distance between the charging point and the connection on the electric vehicle shall not be more than five metre during charging.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The portable socket outlets shall not be permitted for electric vehicle charging.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The lightning protection system shall be provided for the electric vehicle charging stations as per relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The electric vehicle charging station shall be equipped with a protective device against the uncontrolled reverse power flow from electric vehicle to the charging point.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: One second after disconnecting the electric vehicle from the supply mains, the voltage between accessible conductive parts or any accessible conductive part and earth shall be less than or equal to 42.4 V (30 V ), or peak rms 60 V DC, and the stored energy available shall be less than 20 J: Provided that, if the voltage is greater than 42.4 V (30 V ) or 60 V DC, or the energy is 20 J or peak rms

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: more, a warning label shall be attached in a conspicuous position on the charging stations.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: A vehicle connector used for direct current charging shall be locked on the vehicle inlet if the voltage is higher than 60 V DC and in case of charging system malfunction, a means for safe disconnection shall be provided.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The electric vehicle charging point shall disconnect supply of electricity to prevent overvoltage at the battery, if the output voltage exceeds maximum voltage limit permissible for the vehicle.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The electric vehicle charging points shall not energise the charging cable when the vehicle connector is in unlock position.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: The electric vehicle connector shall not unlock if the voltage between the vehicle connector and the earth is more than 60 V.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Safety clearance between the oil or gas dispenser and electric vehicle charging point shall be as per the order issued by the Authority.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Only four core cable shall be used for charging points which require three phase power supply.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Underground cables shall not cross the underground oil tank or oil pipeline.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 124 - General safety requirements for electric vehicle charging station
text: Underground cables through the charging area or vehicles passage shall be avoided and if provided shall be at a minimum depth of one metre from the finished ground surface.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 125 - Earth protection system for the charging station
text: Each electric vehicle charging points shall be supplied individually by a dedicated sub-circuit protected by an overcurrent protective device complying with the relevant standards and the overcurrent protective device shall be part of a switchboard.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 125 - Earth protection system for the charging station
text: Co-ordination of all protective devices in the charging stations shall be ensured.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 125 - Earth protection system for the charging station
text: All electric vehicle charging stations shall be provided with an earth continuity monitoring system that disconnects the supply in the event of the earthing connection to the vehicle becomes ineffective.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 125 - Earth protection system for the charging station
text: The charging lead shall be fitted with an earth-connected metal shielding and the cable insulation shall be wear resistant and maintain flexibility over the operating temperature range.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 125 - Earth protection system for the charging station
text: A protective earth conductor shall be provided to establish an equipotential connection between the earth terminal of the supply and the conductive parts of the vehicle which shall be as per the relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 126 - Requirement to prevent fire for electric vehicle charging station
text: The enclosure of electric vehicle supply equipment shall be made of fire retardant material with self-extinguishing property and free from halogen.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 126 - Requirement to prevent fire for electric vehicle charging station
text: The fire detection, alarm and control system shall be provided as per relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of the charging station shall ensure that the tests as specified in the manufacturer‟s instructions for the residual current devices and the charging station have been carried out.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of the charging station shall keep records of design, construction and labelling to be compatible with a supply of standard voltage at a nominal frequency of 50 Hz of the charging station.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of the charging station shall keep records of the relevant test certificates as indicated in these regulations and as per the relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of the charging station shall keep records of the results of every inspection, testing and periodic assessment and details of any issues observed during the assessment and any actions required to be taken in relation to those issues.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The owner of the charging station shall retain a copy of all records, as specified in sub-regulation (1), (2) and (3) of above, either in hard form or in electronic form, for at least seven years and shall provide a copy of the records to the officials during the inspection.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Additional safety requirements for high voltage direct current

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 129 - Additional safety requirements for high voltage direct current
text: The regulations under this chapter shall be applicable to high voltage direct current which shall be in addition to the regulations provided from chapter I to VII.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A wire mesh shall be provided beneath the walk way; wherever constructed above the hanging valves in the valve hall.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Cables used for sensitive measurements shall be laid in separate and completely screened or covered channels or galvanised steel pipes.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A separate emergency source of illumination with automatic initiation shall be provided in every room or compartment of high voltage direct current station.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No oil immersed apparatus shall be kept within the valve hall.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All doors of compartments containing modules equipped with laser diodes and junction boxes of the fibre optic cables shall be locked and marked with laser warning symbols.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Alternating Current and Direct Current filter banks area shall be efficiently protected by fencing or other means, not less than 1.8 metre in height, so as to prevent access to the conductors and apparatus therein by any unauthorised person and the fencing of such area shall be effictively earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The gate of fencing and earth switch of the filter bank shall be interlocked such that the gate can be opened only after the disconnection and discharging of the filter bank completely.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The air core smoothing reactor shall be fenced all around and the fence shall not fall in the Magnetic Clearance Contour of the smoothing reactor.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Common neutral bus in Alternating Current filters shall be earthed at one point only.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Converter transformer shall be provided with separate perimeter earthing conductor.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The line side neutral of the converter transformer bank shall be earthed at one point only.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Every part of support structure circumference of the air cored reactor shall be earthed in a manner such that it is not forming closed loops.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Radio Frequency Interference screen of valve hall shall be electrically connected and effectively earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Metallic sheet, prefabricated structure members and trusses housing high voltage direct current apparatus shall be electrically connected and effectively earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Insulating pads or sleeves shall be provided between the steel reinforcement components, in foundation beneath the smoothing reactor, wherever they are crossing each other.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Additional safety requirements for gas insulated substation

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
header_path: Section 133 - Additional safety requirements for gas insulated substation
text: The regulations under this chapter shall be applicable to gas insulated substation which shall be in addition to the regulations provided from chapter I to VII.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A separate emergency source of illumination with automatic initiation shall be provided in every room or campartment of gas insulated substation.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Cable cover protection unit shall be provided between flanges of gas insulated substation and cable termination unit.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Gas insulated substation installation of 220 kV and above voltage shall be provided with partial discharge monitoring system.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: SF gas leakage rate from any single compartment of gas insulated substation to atmosphere and between compartments shall not exceed as stipulated in the relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Enclosure of gas insulated substation bay shall be earthed for high frequency transient voltage as per original equipment manufacturer recommendations, apart from the regular earthing.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Earthing of gas insulated substation installation shall be as per relevant standards.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Travelling wave energy generated inside the gas insulated substation due to switching operations shall be diverted to the earth by providing effective earthing from bushing shroud to the earth.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Miscellaneous

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Central Government or the State Government, as the case may be, by an order in writing, allow deviations in respect of matters referred in these regulations except regulation 32.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Electrical Inspector or the Electrical Inspector of mines may, by an order in writing, and reasons to be

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: recorded allow deviation in respect of matters referred in regulations 14 to 19, regulation 30, sub-regulations (2),

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: and (5) of regulation 37, sub-regulation (3) of regulation 38, clauses (i) to (iv) of regulation 39, clause (xii) of regulation 43, regulation 45, sub-regulation (2) of regulation 46, regulations 48, regulations 54 to 56, regulations 59 to 63, regulation 67, regulation 74, regulation 77, regulations 81 to 94, regulation 104, sub- regulations (6), (8) and (10) of regulation 109, regulation 116 and regulations 119 to 135 on case to case basis. Explanation. – Every order allowing the deviations by the Electrical Inspector or the Electrical Inspector of Mines under sub-regulation (2) shall be placed before the Central or State Government which shall have the final decision. Schedule I Handling of electric supply lines and apparatus [See sub-regulation (3) of regulation (21)] Part-I Precautions to be observed: -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Hotline maintenance trained personnel only shall be designated to do work on line.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Work permit shall be taken from the terminal substations at each end of the line.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Work shall be performed with proper planning and prior understanding and clarity.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Favourable climatic condition for hotline operations is sunny weather. If the weather forecasts rain or thunderstorms work will not begin.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Organisation of work shall be discussed among the members and responsibility of each team member fixed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Before going to the work site, all equipment and tools shall be inspected and checked for correct operation.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Auto re-closure shall be in `OFF‟ position for the line at both ends.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The work procedure shall be discussed with the team member at the tower location and the responsibility of each member shall be properly defined.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The land in close vicinity to the tower/poles shall be cleared to provide a site area for the required tools.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All cleaned hot sticks, strain carrier and other assemblies shall be kept on the hotline tool rack to avoid ground contact.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Helmet, safety shoes and safety belt shall compulsorily be used.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All hot sticks and ladders shall be cleaned and checked for integrity by the hot sticks tester.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: All linemen in the hotline team shall be equipped with personal protective equipment during the work.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: No live-line team members on the tower and conductor shall wear any metallic chain, wristwatch or ring to avoid any circulating current.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The team of linemen shall wear conductive socks, boots, helmets and hand gloves. The `hot-end‟ lineman shall wear complete bare hand suit.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Tarpaulin sheet should be laid on the work area.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: A light vehicle shall be kept nearby during entire work period. Tools normally required for hot line maintenance operation: - The following tools conforming to the relevant standards or equivalent specifications shall be used in on-line working:

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wire tongs;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wire tongs saddle;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Tie sticks;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Strain link sticks;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Roller link sticks;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Suspension link sticks;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Auxiliary arms;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Strain carrier;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Gin poles;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Cum-a-along clamp;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Safety equipment like conductor guards, X-arm guards, insulator covers, hand gloves and the like; and

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Hot sticks. Safe Working Distance: - The following safe working distances shall be observed for hot line maintenance operations: Phase to Phase Safe Clearance kV Metre 11 0.61 33 0.71 66 0.91 110 1.02 132 1.07 220 1.52 400 2.13 Handling electric supply lines and apparatus for carrying out shutdown work or testing [See sub-regulation (3) of regulation (21)] Part-II Precautions to be observed: -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Before commencement of any shutdown work or testing in an electric supply line or apparatus, the Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work or testing shall identify the possible hazards, such as; electrocution, flash over, fall of person from height, fall of objects from height, failure of tools and plants, fire, and the like, that may be encountered while carrying out the work or testing near charged area and take necessary precaution to protect the working personnel.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall, before commencement of any work, brief the entire working group or gang regarding the hazards that may be encountered and the necessary precautions to be taken by them.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall obtain proper permit-to-work from the concerned Operation In-charge(s) and ensure that the electric supply line or apparatus or section is isolated from all sources of energy, de-energised and earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall ensure that adequate and appropriate local earths are fixed at the zone of working, and the earthing rods remain connected to the isolated section of the electric supply line or apparatus or section till all men and materials have been moved away to safe zone and permit to work is returned on completion of the work.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: If the local earths are required to be removed for any testing purpose, the same shall be done only when all the working personnel are in the safe zone, on the ground or on the tower, and in the presence of the Engineer or Supervisor. If the working personnel are required to go up or approach the conductor(s) subsequently for any work, such as removal of test leads, tightening or adjustment, they shall be permitted to proceed only after re-fixing the local earths, as required.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall positively confirm by suitable means that the electric supply line or apparatus or section is totally dead before giving clearance for the working personnel to approach the same.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall, while carrying out the shutdown work or testing, ensure that working personnel are maintaining safe distance from the adjacent charged electric supply line or apparatus or section, and also, no objects, such as tools and plants, ladders, cranes, man-lifts, and the like, are moved, so as to infringe the safe distance, endangering the working personnel.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Mobile cranes, derricks, man lifts and wheel mounted ladders shall be effectively earthed when being moved or

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: operated in close proximity with energised apparatus or section.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Portable ladders and poles shall be carried only in the horizontal position when being moved in close proximity with energised lines or equipment or area. Further Precautions to be observed: -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Adequate and effective supervision shall be ensured by the owner as well as the contractor for all activities while working or testing on electric supply lines and apparatus when any shutdown work or testing is done near charged electric supply line or apparatus or section.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Lone worker shall never be allowed to work on electric supply lines, equipment and apparatus or while testing.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Sufficient supervisory personnel shall be deployed for close monitoring while various type of works are under progress at the same or different locations. Supervising work shall never be delegated to the sub-contractors' personnel.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The deployed Supervising Personnel shall not leave the working spot when shutdown work at height or testing is in the progress, as the working personnel may not be aware of the consequences of unsafe practices. No other work, which requires them to move out of the location, shall be undertaken by Supervising personnel, when shutdown work or testing is in the progress.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wherever shutdown activities are required to be carried out for more than one day on any electric supply lines, apparatus or section, earthing provided at the said work site shall be inspected by the Engineer or Supervisor everyday morning for their healthiness, fitness and proper tightening, before giving clearance for the working personnel to climb the tower or structure to resume the work. Handling high voltage direct current apparatus for carrying out shutdown work or testing [See sub-regulation (3) of regulation (21)] Part-III Precautions to be observed: -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall obtain proper Permit-To-Work (PTW) from the concerned Operation In-charge(s) and ensure that the electric supply line or apparatus or section is isolated from all sources of energy, de-energised and earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Before commencement of any shutdown work or testing of high voltage direct current apparatus, the Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work or testing shall identify the possible hazards, such as; electrocution, flash over, fall of person from height, fall of objects from height, failure of tools and plants, fire, and the like, that may be encountered while carrying out the work or testing near charged area and take necessary precaution to protect the working personnel.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall, before commencement of any work, brief the entire working group or gang of the hazards that may be encountered and the necessary precautions to be taken by them.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Attach warning labels to all neighboring installation parts (to be removed after the works have been carried out).

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall allow access to the valve hall, DC Filter Area, AC Filter Area and DC hall (if any) only when the apparatus therein are completely de-energised and effectively earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The work on AC/DC filter bank shall only begin after earthing the entire capacitor bank.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: There shall be at least ten minute time gap between earthing the entire capacitor bank and starting the work on bank. There after unit must be short circuited.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The de-energised bushing shall be checked for stored charge by touching all the surfaces of both indoor and outdoor side composite insulators and all other parts of the bushing using a proper test instrument.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The gas pressure inside high voltage direct current through wall bushing shall be reduced to a level prescribed by the manufacturer before starting any work or handling of the bushing. Handling Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) apparatus for carrying out shutdown work or testing [See sub-regulation (3) of regulation (21)] Part-IV Precautions to be observed: -

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall obtain proper Permit-To-Work from the concerned Operation In-charge(s) and ensure that the electric supply line or apparatus or section is isolated from all sources of energy, de-energised and earthed.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Operation, maintenance and repair must be carried out by trained and certified personnel only.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Before commencement of any shutdown work or testing of gas insulated switchgear apparatus, the Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work or testing shall identify the possible hazards, such as; electrocution, flash over, fall of person from height, fall of objects from height, failure of Tools and Plants, fire, and the like, that may be encountered while carrying out the work or testing near charged area and take necessary precaution to protect the working personnel.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall, before commencement of any work, brief the entire working group or gang of the hazards that may be encountered and the necessary precautions to be taken by them.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wear hearing protection during operation.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Take care while touching the enclosure at any time as enclosures may heat up to the temperature of 70°C.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Observe the procedures for storage, transportation, and the use of filling equipment.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Wear the personal protective equipment: respirator mask (self-contained breathing equipment if necessary), protective overall, protective gloves, safety shoes, safety glasses.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Attach warning labels to all neighboring installation parts (to be removed after the works have been carried out).

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Provide proper electrical clearance as required by interlocking rules. Mark e.g. main circuits and control circuits with appropriate tags.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Block off neighboring live parts with screens, insulating mats or spacer grids in order to prevent unintended contacts.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: While working on any compartment in gas insulated switchgear, the immediate adjacent compartment(s) must be also depressurised for safety of the working person.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: SF gas becomes contaminated and contains poisonous substances after events such as arc faults. Hence, handling of SF in such cases must be done using proper PPEs and by a trained personnel, preferably from the original equipment manufacturer.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The switchgear installation shall not be operated if the density of SF gas indicated at the density monitors is not in the operating range.

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: Do not remove any protective covers if an assembly is energized.

text: The Engineer or Supervisor in-charge of the work shall ensure that adequate and appropriate local earths are fixed at the zone of working, and the earthing rods remain connected to the isolated section of the electric supply line or apparatus or section till all men and materials have been moved away to safe zone and Permit-To-Work is returned on completion of the work. (For Self-certification by supplier /owner /consumer) CERTIFICATE [Under Regulation (32) and (45) of CEA (Measures relating to Safety & Electricity Supply) Regulation, 2023] This is to certify that the electrical installation is complete in all respects and the work has been carried out conforming to the CEA (Measures relating to Safety & Electric Supply) Regulation, 2023 and relevant standards. The site tests done are found to be in order and it is electrically safe to operate the apparatus free from any danger.

text: “Safety Working Clearance” is the minimum clearance to be maintained in air between the live part of the equipment on one hand and earth or another piece of equipment or conductor on which it is necessary to carry out the work, on the other;

source_framework: Measures relating to Safety and Electric Supply - Directorate General Of Mines Safety (DGMS) Guidelines
text: The “Highest System Voltage” is defined as the highest rms phase to phase voltage which occurs under normal operating conditions at any time and at any point of the system. It excludes voltage transients (such as those due to system switching) and temporary voltage variations due to abnormal system conditions (such as those due to fault conditions or the sudden disconnection of large loads).
